name: hypothesis-generation
description: Structured hypothesis formulation from observations. Use when you have experimental observations or data and need to formulate testable hypotheses with predictions, propose mechanisms, and design experiments to test them. Follows scientific method framework. For open-ended ideation use scientific-brainstorming; for automated LLM-driven hypothesis testing on datasets use hypogenic.
capabilities: Formulate 3-5 competing mechanistic hypotheses from observations
Design specific experiments to test each hypothesis
Evaluate hypothesis quality against scientific criteria
Synthesize supporting evidence from literature search
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown files, LaTeX templates, and reference guides. No executable code exists. All 273 static findings are false positives: backticks in markdown code blocks were misidentified as shell commands, RGB color definitions were misidentified as cryptographic algorithms, and file references were misidentified as reconnaissance.
valueStatement: Transform observations into structured, testable scientific hypotheses. This skill guides you through literature synthesis, mechanism development, experimental design, and prediction formulation using proven scientific method frameworks.
limitations: Does not perform actual experiments or access external databases
Requires user to provide domain knowledge and observations
Output is a structured report, not executable research
Does not replace peer review or expert consultation

useCases.title: Research Proposal Development
useCases.description: Develop rigorous hypotheses for thesis proposals with proper experimental designs and literature grounding.

useCases.title: Grant Proposal Hypotheses
useCases.description: Structure compelling testable hypotheses with clear predictions for funding applications.

useCases.title: Hypothesis-Driven Analysis
useCases.description: Formulate hypotheses from exploratory data analysis before confirmatory statistical testing.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Hypothesis
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the hypothesis-generation skill to create 3-5 testable hypotheses for the following phenomenon: [describe observation or data pattern]. Include mechanistic explanations, supporting evidence from literature, and experimental designs to test each hypothesis.

promptTemplates.title: Mechanism-Focused
promptTemplates.prompt: Apply the hypothesis-generation skill to explain the mechanism of [phenomenon]. Generate competing mechanistic hypotheses, design experiments to distinguish between them, and propose specific quantitative predictions for each.

promptTemplates.title: Literature-Grounded
promptTemplates.prompt: Use hypothesis-generation to develop novel hypotheses about [research question]. Ground each hypothesis in evidence from PubMed literature, identify gaps in current understanding, and design experiments that would advance the field.

promptTemplates.title: Full Report
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a comprehensive hypothesis report using the hypothesis-generation skill. Include: 3-5 competing hypotheses with mechanistic explanations, testable predictions in amber boxes, comparison boxes showing how to distinguish hypotheses, and detailed experimental designs in appendices. Use the LaTeX template format.

bestPractices: Start with comprehensive literature search to ground hypotheses in existing evidence before formulation
Ensure each hypothesis makes distinct, testable predictions that can distinguish it from alternatives
Include falsification criteria for every prediction to maintain scientific rigor
antiPatterns: Generating vague hypotheses without specific quantitative predictions
Ignoring contradictory evidence or alternative explanations
Skipping the experimental design phase - hypotheses must be testable

faq.question: What is the difference between this skill and scientific-brainstorming?
faq.answer: Hypothesis-generation follows a structured scientific method with formal hypothesis criteria. Brainstorming is open-ended ideation without testing requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill search PubMed automatically?
faq.answer: No - you must use WebFetch or WebSearch tools to retrieve literature. The skill provides search strategy guidance in references.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-biological sciences?
faq.answer: Yes - the framework applies to any scientific domain including physics, chemistry, psychology, and social sciences.

faq.question: What format does the output use?
faq.answer: Primary output is LaTeX using provided templates. Alternative formats can be requested by specifying your preference.

faq.question: How many hypotheses should I generate?
faq.answer: The skill recommends 3-5 competing hypotheses - enough for meaningful comparison without overwhelming analysis.

faq.question: What makes a hypothesis testable?
faq.answer: A testable hypothesis has specific predictions that can be measured, falsification criteria if wrong, and feasible experimental methods.
